metadata.name: langevals-example
metadata.summary: LangEvals boilerplate example evaluator for LLMs.

classes.name: langevals_example.word_count.ExampleWordCountEntry
documentation: Type definition of what keys are necessary for each entry to have for the evaluator to process it, in this example
we only need the `output` to be available, options are `input`, `output`, `contexts` and `expected_output`

classes.name: langevals_example.word_count.ExampleWordCountSettings
documentation: Generic settings for the evaluator, in this example we don't need any settings, but any fields can be added here

classes.name: langevals_example.word_count.ExampleWordCountResult
documentation: The structure that holds the result of the evaluation, if a score is used, it's a good idea to overwrite the
EvaluationResult class to add a pydantic description to the field explaning what the score means for this evaluator,

classes.name: langevals_example.word_count.ExampleWordCountEvaluator

class_variables.name: langevals_example.word_count.ExampleWordCountEvaluator.category
documentation: The category of the evaluator, can be "safety", "quality", "other", etc, check BaseEvaluator for all options

class_variables.name: langevals_example.word_count.ExampleWordCountEvaluator.env_vars
documentation: The environment variables that are necessary for the evaluator to run

class_variables.name: langevals_example.word_count.ExampleWordCountEvaluator.default_settings
documentation: The default settings for the evaluator in case no settings are provided

class_variables.name: langevals_example.word_count.ExampleWordCountEvaluator.docs_url
documentation: The URL to the official documentation of the evaluator

class_variables.name: langevals_example.word_count.ExampleWordCountEvaluator.is_guardrail
documentation: If the evaluator is a guardrail or not, a guardrail evaluator must return a boolean result on the `passed` result field in addition to the score
This evaluator serves as a boilerplate for creating new evaluators.
